mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and tighten financial conditions, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hike periods with statistical significance (p < 0.05), or rolling Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the wrong direction.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals an impending recession, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts indicates a synchronized collapse in consumer demand and residential investment, which forces equity markets to reprice growth expectations downward over the following month.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 20-day returns, with a positive mean return observed in 55% of co-occurring decline events.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a rebound in the index of industrial production signals resilient manufacturing output that offsets rate hike headwinds, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO rebounds during tightening cycles are followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the conditional predictive accuracy drops below baseline levels.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth correlates with negative 20-day forward returns in over 60% of observations, or rolling Rank IC is consistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index annualized growth rate crosses above 4.0%, inflationary pressures force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of periods where CPIAUCSL growth surpasses 4.0%, invalidating the inflation penalty mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Co-occurring increases in GDPC1 and RSAFS are followed by negative 20-day returns in over 50% of cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a contraction in industrial production signals deteriorating manufacturing demand that precedes equity market corrections, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions in late-cycle phases are followed by positive 20-day returns in over 60% of instances, or the signal fails to achieve a positive Rank IC in walk-forward validation.
